content: I've been thinking a lot about how our tech industry impacts the environment. As a QA lead at a healthcare company, I see so much electronic waste from outdated equipment.
That's a really important issue. What kinds of environmental initiatives has your company implemented?
We've started a recycling program for old devices, but it's not enough. I'm 59 and I've noticed how much more waste there is compared to when I started in tech 30 years ago.
That's a significant amount of time in the industry. How have you seen technology's environmental impact evolve over those decades?
It's gotten much worse, honestly. I used to work at a startup in Austin where we'd upgrade devices every year. Now I'm in Minneapolis and trying to be more conscious about consumption.
The shift from Austin to Minneapolis must have been quite a change. How are you finding the adjustment?
It's been good, though I'm still recovering from a knee injury I got last winter. I used to do Brazilian jiu-jitsu regularly, but I've had to slow down.
I'm sorry to hear about your injury. That must be frustrating, especially with an activity you enjoy.
It is, but I'm staying active with speedcubing and crossword puzzles. I can do those while sitting down. Plus, I've gotten really into podcasts about environmental science.
Speedcubing is such a fascinating hobby! And podcasts are a great way to stay informed while recovering. Any particular environmental topics you've been learning about?

Mostly ocean pollution and sustainable tech. I've been thinking about how we could make our testing processes more environmentally friendly. Maybe virtual testing environments instead of physical devices?
That's a great idea! Virtual testing could significantly reduce electronic waste. Have you proposed this to your team?
Not yet, but I'm working on a proposal. I'm also part of a board game group that meets weekly, and we've started using digital versions to reduce paper waste.
That's a clever way to combine your hobbies with environmental consciousness. Do you find the digital board games as satisfying as physical ones?
Surprisingly yes! The apps have gotten really good. Oh, and I almost forgot - I have a massive aquarium at home with some rare tropical fish. It's become a bit of a passion project.
An aquarium sounds wonderful! It must be quite a commitment to maintain. How do you balance that with your other interests?
It takes time, but it's worth it. I've been researching sustainable fishkeeping practices lately. Did you know some species are overharvested for the aquarium trade?
I wasn't aware of that. It's interesting how our hobbies can connect to larger environmental issues. Your aquarium must give you a unique perspective on marine conservation.
